Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Change Venue; Memorandum In Support Of Motion For Change Of Venue; Declarations Of Khursheed Husain And Craig R. Smith In Support Of Motion
Off Calendar Jun-16-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS SYED HUSAIN and KHURSHEED HUSAIN'S Motion To Change Venue.

Defendants' "motion to change venue" of this attorney fee dispute is denied. While conceding that "ordinarily the convenience of parties is not taken into consideration," defendants' sole ground for their motion is defendant Kursheed Husain's convenience. Husain's declares that her "medical necessities" are a "hinderance" to a trial in San Francisco. While this court is sympathetic, Husain does not explain how the hinderance would be less in neighboring San Mateo County. Husain reports spending much of her time at Stanford Medical Center, which is farther from her Hillsborough house than the San Francisco civil courthouse. Moreover, the parties' papers show activities in several locales. Husain's declaration, which asserts residence in Nevada and San Mateo County, was signed at Stanford, California (Santa Clara County). And Marin County was venue for the suit defendants brought that gave rise to the fee dispute.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
